reason: Mislocated convergence / type mismatch. The series sum_k C(-1/2,k) U^k is a series whose terms U^k are OPERATORS, i.e. elements of End(B(H)_sa) (U=S^2-I acts on B(H)_sa). The node asserts it 'converges absolutely ... in the complete order-unit space'. That is the wrong space: the order-unit space is B(H)_sa (whose elements are operators a, not maps), and GT-order-unit-norm (refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372) only equips B(H)_sa with the order norm. The convergence actually takes place in the Banach algebra of bounded operators on B(H)_sa, which is exactly the setting of GT-kitaev-spectral (refs/kitaev:503: 'Let calB be a Banach algebra ...'). The required fact -- that End(B(H)_sa) with the induced operator norm is a complete Banach algebra (so the absolutely convergent series converges to an element R) -- is neither GT-order-unit-norm nor stated/provenanced in the node. As written, R is constructed in the wrong space and the completeness needed for convergence is unprovenanced.
